channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Happy, who taught researchers that elephants can recognize themselves, is euthanized
item.description: Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55.
item.content:encoded:
Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55.

(Image credit: Bebeto Matthews)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ex-CIA official charged with stealing millions of dollars in gold bars
item.description: A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.
item.content:encoded:
A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades netting $1.2 million
item.description: It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.
item.content:encoded:
It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.

(Image credit: Josh Edelson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: An art museum in Maine tries to balance protecting art and saving energy
item.description: Keeping a museum's temperature and humidity constant -- rain or shine, all year long -- takes a massive amount of energy, and it's expensive. But some museums have a solution.
item.content:encoded: Keeping a museum's temperature and humidity constant -- rain or shine, all year long -- takes a massive amount of energy, and it's expensive. But some museums have a solution.
item.dc:creator: Molly Enking

item.title: A National Spelling Bee champion shares what it takes to win
item.description: The 2025 Scripps National Spelling Bee champ shares what it takes to spell it right in 2026.
item.content:encoded: The 2025 Scripps National Spelling Bee champ shares what it takes to spell it right in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Visual impairments don't keep these birders out of the hobby
item.description: The phrase "bird watching" does not take in the full range of people who love searching for wild birds. We meet a few of the many visually impaired birders who use their ears.
item.content:encoded: The phrase "bird watching" does not take in the full range of people who love searching for wild birds. We meet a few of the many visually impaired birders who use their ears.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Eve Cohen

item.title: A wildfire off the California coast threatened one of the world's rarest pines
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Heather Schneider of the Santa Barbara Botanic Garden about the garden's efforts to conserve seeds of rare plants from Santa Rosa Island, where a wildfire just burned.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Heather Schneider of the Santa Barbara Botanic Garden about the garden's efforts to conserve seeds of rare plants from Santa Rosa Island, where a wildfire just burned.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: Immigration detention center known as 'Alligator Alcatraz' may soon close
item.description: A detention center in the Florida Everglades dubbed "Alligator Alcatraz" has become too expensive to maintain and may soon close.
item.content:encoded: A detention center in the Florida Everglades dubbed "Alligator Alcatraz" has become too expensive to maintain and may soon close.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bowman

item.title: In a village in rural Alaska, these kids' school bus is an airplane
item.description: In most school districts, kids take a bus to school. But in the rural Alaska village of South Naknek -- pilot Jon King has been flying kids to school almost every school day for the last four decades.
item.content:encoded: In most school districts, kids take a bus to school. But in the rural Alaska village of South Naknek -- pilot Jon King has been flying kids to school almost every school day for the last four decades.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Cassandra

item.title: Trump's third check-up at Walter Reed raises more questions about his health
item.description: The White House is saying little about President Trump's check-up at Walter Reed Tuesday, his third in 13 months. Critics say the lack of transparency only raises more questions about his health.
item.content:encoded: The White House is saying little about President Trump's check-up at Walter Reed Tuesday, his third in 13 months. Critics say the lack of transparency only raises more questions about his health.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram
